title: Cloud Storage Overview
content: Data is the most valuable asset in the cloud. Cloud storage provides scalable, resilient, and secure ways to store your data, whether it's for a high-performance database, a static website, or long-term backups.
insight: Choosing the right storage type is critical for performance and cost.

title: Block Storage (EBS)
content: Block storage provides raw storage volumes that you can attach to an instance. It behaves like a physical hard drive. In AWS, this is called Elastic Block Store (EBS).
insight: Block storage is for data that changes frequently, like operating systems or databases.

title: EBS: SSD vs. HDD
content: EBS volumes come in different types: SSD-backed volumes (gp3, io2) are for transactional workloads, while HDD-backed volumes (st1, sc1) are for large, sequential workloads like log processing.
insight: SSD for speed; HDD for volume and throughput.

title: Provisioned IOPS
content: IOPS (Input/Output Operations Per Second) measures how fast you can read and write to a disk. For mission-critical databases, you can 'provision' a specific amount of IOPS to guarantee performance.
insight: Pay for the exact performance your database needs.

title: EBS Snapshots
content: Snapshots are point-in-time backups of your EBS volumes. They are stored incrementally in S3, meaning you only pay for the data that has changed since the last snapshot.
insight: Snapshots are your safety net for block storage.

title: Object Storage (Amazon S3)
content: Simple Storage Service (S3) is an object storage service that offers industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance. Unlike block storage, you access objects via a URL.
insight: S3 is the 'bottomless' storage bucket of the internet.

title: Buckets and Objects
content: In S3, data is stored in 'buckets'. A bucket is a container for 'objects'. An object consists of the file itself, its metadata, and a unique identifier (the key).
insight: Bucket names must be globally unique across all AWS accounts.

title: S3 Standard
content: The default storage class for frequently accessed data. It offers high durability, availability, and performance.
insight: Use S3 Standard for active files that need immediate access.

title: S3 Intelligent-Tiering
content: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves your data between two access tiers (frequent and infrequent) when access patterns change, saving you money automatically.
insight: Let AWS optimize your storage costs for you.

title: S3 Standard-IA
content: For data that is accessed less frequently but requires rapid access when needed. It has a lower storage price but a higher retrieval fee.
insight: Perfect for backups or older data that you might still need quickly.

title: S3 Glacier
content: Glacier is for long-term data archiving. Retrieval times can range from minutes to hours, but the storage cost is extremely low.
insight: Glacier is the 'cold storage' for data you rarely look at.

title: The 'Eleven Nines' (Durability)
content: S3 is designed for 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability. This means if you store 10 million objects, you can expect to lose one object every 10,000 years.
insight: Your data is safer in S3 than in your own data center.

title: S3 Versioning
content: Versioning allows you to keep multiple variants of an object in the same bucket. You can use it to recover objects from accidental deletion or overwrite.
insight: Versioning is the 'Undo' button for your S3 objects.

title: Lifecycle Policies
content: Lifecycle policies allow you to automatically transition objects to cheaper storage classes or delete them after a certain period of time.
insight: Automate your data's journey from 'hot' to 'cold' to 'deleted'.

title: Static Website Hosting
content: S3 can host static websites (HTML, CSS, JS, images) without a server. It is highly scalable and very inexpensive.
insight: The easiest way to host a modern frontend application.

title: Elastic File System (EFS)
content: EFS provides a simple, scalable, fully managed elastic NFS file system. Unlike EBS, EFS can be mounted by hundreds of instances simultaneously.
insight: EFS is a 'shared drive' for your cloud servers.

title: Instance Store
content: Instance store provides temporary block-level storage for your instance. This storage is located on disks that are physically attached to the host computer. Data is lost if the instance is stopped or terminated.
insight: Use instance store for scratch space or temporary caches only.

title: Cloud Data Transfer
content: Inbound data transfer is usually free. Outbound data transfer (egress) to the internet or across regions usually incurs a cost. Transfers within the same AZ are often free.
insight: Watch your outbound traffic; it's a common hidden cost.

title: AWS Storage Gateway
content: Storage Gateway connects an on-premises software appliance with cloud-based storage to provide seamless integration between your data center and the cloud.
insight: A bridge for moving your local data to the cloud.

title: The Snow Family
content: Devices like Snowball and Snowcone are physical hardware used to migrate massive amounts of data (petabytes) into the cloud when the internet is too slow.
insight: Sometimes the fastest way to move data is by truck.

title: AWS FSx
content: FSx makes it easy to launch and run popular file systems like Windows File Server and Lustre in the cloud.
insight: Managed file systems for specific enterprise workloads.

title: Relational Database Service (RDS)
content: RDS is a managed service that makes it easy to set up, operate, and scale a relational database (like MySQL, PostgreSQL, or SQL Server) in the cloud.
insight: Let AWS manage patches and backups for your SQL databases.

title: Amazon DynamoDB
content: A key-value and document database that delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale. It is fully managed and serverless.
insight: NoSQL storage that scales to millions of requests per second.

title: Amazon Aurora
content: A MySQL and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built for the cloud that combines the performance of high-end commercial databases with the simplicity of open source.
insight: Aurora is AWS's premium, cloud-native relational database.

title: Bucket Policies
content: Bucket policies are JSON-based access policy language that you can use to add or deny permissions to your S3 buckets and the objects in them.
insight: Control access to your data at the bucket level.

title: S3 Public Access Block
content: A critical security feature that provides a centralized control to prevent the accidental exposure of S3 buckets to the public internet.
insight: Always enable 'Block Public Access' unless you are hosting a public website.

title: Encryption at Rest
content: Encryption at rest protects your data by encrypting it while it's stored on disk. Cloud providers offer managed keys (KMS) to make this easy.
insight: Encrypted data is useless to anyone who steals the physical disk.

title: Encryption in Transit
content: Encryption in transit (using SSL/TLS) ensures your data is encrypted while it travels over the network between your computer and the cloud.
insight: Always use HTTPS to talk to cloud storage APIs.

title: EFS Performance Modes
content: EFS offers 'General Purpose' for most workloads and 'Max I/O' for large-scale, highly parallelized workloads.
insight: Choose the right performance mode for your file sharing needs.

title: AWS Backup
content: A centralized backup service that makes it easy to automate and consolidate backup tasks across AWS services for compute, storage, and databases.
insight: One tool to rule all your cloud backups.

title: S3 Object Lock
content: Object Lock prevents objects from being deleted or overwritten for a fixed amount of time. This is useful for compliance and protection against ransomware.
insight: Write Once, Read Many (WORM) storage for critical data.

title: S3 Select
content: S3 Select allows you to use SQL statements to filter the contents of an S3 object and retrieve only the subset of data that you need.
insight: Retrieve only the data you need, reducing transfer costs and time.

title: EBS Multi-Attach
content: Multi-attach allows you to attach a single Provisioned IOPS SSD volume to multiple EC2 instances in the same AZ.
insight: Useful for clustered applications that need shared block storage.

title: S3 Replication
content: S3 can automatically replicate objects across buckets in different regions (CRR) or the same region (SRR).
insight: Perfect for global data distribution and disaster recovery.

title: S3 Transfer Acceleration
content: Uses Edge Locations to provide fast, easy, and secure transfers of files over long distances between your client and an S3 bucket.
insight: Speed up uploads from around the world by using the AWS backbone.

title: Elastic Volumes
content: EBS Elastic Volumes allow you to increase volume size, change volume type, or adjust performance while the volume is still attached and in use.
insight: Grow your storage without taking your server offline.

title: S3 Pre-Signed URLs
content: A pre-signed URL gives temporary access to an object in S3 without requiring AWS credentials. You can set an expiration time for the link.
insight: The best way to share private files securely with users.

title: Amazon Athena
content: An interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data directly in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. There is no infrastructure to manage.
insight: Query your 'S3 data lake' as if it were a database.

title: Amazon Redshift
content: A fast, fully managed data warehouse that makes it simple and cost-effective to analyze all your data using standard SQL and your existing Business Intelligence (BI) tools.
insight: Storage for massive analytical workloads.

title: Conclusion
content: Cloud storage is about much more than just saving files. It's about building a durable, scalable, and automated data foundation for your entire application stack.
insight: Automate your storage lifecycle to keep performance high and costs low.
